English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Ray Wu papers
Wu, Ray
21-47-3771
26 cubic feet. 26 cubic feet.
[ca. 1966-2007] Correspondence, Manuscripts, Photographs, Printed Materials, Publications, Research Materials Professional papers journal articles and reprints, manuscript reviews, thesis reviews, course material, photographs and correspondence of Professor Ray Wu.. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 27 ======= 27 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 6741752
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Dhaval Mehta
Date completed:
March 2011
EAD encoding:
Dhaval Mehta, March 2011
Date modified:
RMC Staff, September 2013
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Molecular Biology and Genetics. Dr. Wu developed the first method for sequencing DNA and some of the fundamental tools for DNA cloning in 1970. He led research on genetic modification of rice and other crops to increase resistance to pests, drought, heat, salt, and other environmental stresses. Dr. Wu died in Ithaca on February 10, 2008.
Ray Wu was born in Beijing in 1928. He received his undergraduate degree from the University of Alabama and his Ph.D. in biochemistry from the University of Pennsylvania in 1955. From 1955 to 1966, Dr. Wu conducted research at the Public Health Research Institute of the City of New York. He came to Cornell in 1966 as an associate professor of biochemistry and molecular biology, became a professor in 1972, and in 2004 was named the Liberty Hyde Bailey Professor of Molecular Biology and Genetics. He served as department chair in the Section of Biochemistry, Molecular and Cell Biology. He founded the China-United States Biochemistry and Molecular Biology Examination and Application Program, which from 1982 to 1989, brought over 400 of the top Chinese students to the U.S. for graduate training. While on sabbatical leave in 1989, he served as director of the Institute of Molecular Biology of Academia Sinica in Taipei, Taiwan. He also served as an honorary professor at Peking University. He held many advisory roles to the Chinese and Taiwanese governments and as science advisor to the China National Center for Biotechnology Development, and chaired the Scientific Advisory Committee of BioAgricultural Sciences (Taiwan), the Advisory Committee to the Transgenic Plan Program, National Science Council (Taiwan), and the Board of Scientific Advisors of the International Center for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology. He was elected a fellow of the American Academy for the Advancement of Science in 2003; and elected a fellow in the Chinese Academy of Engineering.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Professional papers journal articles and reprints, manuscript reviews, thesis reviews, course material, photographs and correspondence of Professor Ray Wu..
General
Some folders have been removed from this collection because of their content. In most, but not all cases, this is indicated in the guide below.
Cite As:
Ray Wu papers, #21-47-3771 . Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Plant genetic engineering. Crops -- Genetic engineering. Transgenic plants. Genetic engineering. China United States Biochemistry Examination and Application. Cornell University. Department of Molecular Biology and Genetics Zhongguo ke xue yuan
6741752 <<<<<<< HEAD Reprints I: Academic Papers and Publications, Symposium Proceedings, Conference Reports, Newsletters.1
Scope and Contents
Includes papers on rice genetics, and natural and agricultural biotechnology.Reprints II: Academic Papers and Publications, Symposium Proceedings, Conference Reports and Correspondence2
Scope and Contents
Reprints include papers on rice genetics. Correspondence includes that with Post Doctoral candidates and applicants, and that concerning Academia Sinica, TaipeiCorrespondence3

Scope and Contents
Includes correspondence with students, colleagues, co-workers, scientists, corporations, universities in China, and friends. Scope and Contents
Dissertations include those from Indian institute of Science, National Chung Hsing University, Taiwan, Academia Sinica, Taiwan, Purdue university, University of California at Santa Barbara and Cornell University, Publications include Agro Food Hitech, AgBiotech '98, The CAAS Journal, China Science and Technology Newsletter, Committee of Hundred Survey, The Rice Foundation, The Plant Journal, Plant Physiology, Texas Rice, IRRI Research Notes, Rice Today, Chinese Rice Research Newsletter, Engineering Science Quarterly, Chinese Academy of Engineers Newsletter and Plant Molecular Biology. Scope and Contents
Reports include those by the Cornell Genomics Initiative Task Force, Maw Ye and Valerie Jean Karpus. Publications and journals include Proceedings of the 22nd Rice Technical Working Group, Nutritional Disorders of the Rice Plant in Asia, National Health Research Institutes (China), Plant Molecular Genetics and Breeding, Biotechnology in China, Reminiscences and Perspectives (Academia Sinica), Prioritizing the Rice Biotechnology Research Agenda for Eastern India, Institute of Botany (Academia Sinica), etc. Abstracts include those from the 4th International Rice Functional Genomics Symposium, Molecular Biology in the 21st Century (Academia Sinica), Plant Molecular Biology Association Conference, 2nd International Symposium of Rice Functional Genomics, etc. Reprints include SRI - the System of Rice Intensification a glossary of plant terms and miscellaneous journal articles. Lecture material includes notes and slides for BioBM 737.Reprints of Journal Articles1959-19841997-199925
Scope and Contents
Reprints of articles authored/co-authored by Ray Wu from Molecular Biology, Plant Molecular Biology, Gene, Genes and Development, Molecular and Cellular Biology, Methods in Enzymology, Molecular and General Genetics, Biochemistry, The Journal of Biological Chemistry, Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA, Nature, Science, Journal of Virology, Nucleic Acids Research, Gene Function, Biotechnology in Agriculture and Forestry, Plant Biotechnology, Plant Physiology etc.Notes, Reports and Reprints: Post Doctoral Candidates1975-1986261BS 330 Problem SetsApril 1981262Notes and Lecture Material: BioBM 7372000263Rotation Report: Min Hue ChenFebruary 1987264Seminar Notes: Shanghai; Lecture Material and Slides1980265Box 2727
Conditions Governing Access
Restricted ======= Reprints I: Academic Papers and Publications, Symposium Proceedings, Conference Reports, Newsletters.1
Scope and Contents
Includes papers on rice genetics, and natural and agricultural biotechnology.Reprints II: Academic Papers and Publications, Symposium Proceedings, Conference Reports and Correspondence2
Scope and Contents
Reprints include papers on rice genetics. Correspondence includes that with Post Doctoral candidates and applicants, and that concerning Academia Sinica, TaipeiCorrespondence3

Scope and Contents
Dissertations include those from Indian institute of Science, National Chung Hsing University, Taiwan, Academia Sinica, Taiwan, Purdue university, University of California at Santa Barbara and Cornell University, Publications include Agro Food Hitech, AgBiotech '98, The CAAS Journal, China Science and Technology Newsletter, Committee of Hundred Survey, The Rice Foundation, The Plant Journal, Plant Physiology, Texas Rice, IRRI Research Notes, Rice Today, Chinese Rice Research Newsletter, Engineering Science Quarterly, Chinese Academy of Engineers Newsletter and Plant Molecular Biology. Scope and Contents
Reports include those by the Cornell Genomics Initiative Task Force, Maw Ye and Valerie Jean Karpus. Publications and journals include Proceedings of the 22nd Rice Technical Working Group, Nutritional Disorders of the Rice Plant in Asia, National Health Research Institutes (China), Plant Molecular Genetics and Breeding, Biotechnology in China, Reminiscences and Perspectives (Academia Sinica), Prioritizing the Rice Biotechnology Research Agenda for Eastern India, Institute of Botany (Academia Sinica), etc. Abstracts include those from the 4th International Rice Functional Genomics Symposium, Molecular Biology in the 21st Century (Academia Sinica), Plant Molecular Biology Association Conference, 2nd International Symposium of Rice Functional Genomics, etc. Reprints include SRI - the System of Rice Intensification a glossary of plant terms and miscellaneous journal articles. Lecture material includes notes and slides for BioBM 737.Reprints of Journal Articles1959-19841997-199925
Scope and Contents
Reprints of articles authored/co-authored by Ray Wu from Molecular Biology, Plant Molecular Biology, Gene, Genes and Development, Molecular and Cellular Biology, Methods in Enzymology, Molecular and General Genetics, Biochemistry, The Journal of Biological Chemistry, Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA, Nature, Science, Journal of Virology, Nucleic Acids Research, Gene Function, Biotechnology in Agriculture and Forestry, Plant Biotechnology, Plant Physiology etc.Notes, Reports and Reprints: Post Doctoral Candidates1975-1986261BS 330 Problem SetsApril 1981262Notes and Lecture Material: BioBM 7372000263Rotation Report: Min Hue ChenFebruary 1987264Seminar Notes: Shanghai; Lecture Material and Slides1980265Box 2727
